body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: Librarian memory service wired
body.intention: I intend that the Librarian device is the authoritative owner of memory write/retrieval, with edge maintenance wired through it.
body.description:
D-shared-memory-service-2026-05-28 shipped T-memory-agent-write-api, T-librarian-edge-maintenance, T-librarian-retrieval-service (all closed), but no consequence check was run.

**What changed:** Librarian was designated as the always-on memory service owner — write API, edge maintenance, and retrieval all route through Librarian.

**Predicted unintended effects:** Tickets may have added the API surfaces without fully wiring callers through them. Igor may still be doing direct DB writes instead of calling Librarian's write API.

**Signals to watch:** Check if memory writes (cortex.store, interpretive_edge inserts) go through Librarian's write API or directly to DB. Check if edge maintenance (Hebbian decay, orphan rescue) is triggered by Librarian lifecycle, not Igor's NE cycle directly.

**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-23

**Affected files:** None — observation and code-review only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Code audit: verify callers route through Librarian service, not direct DB. Out of scope: performance, new features.
**Test plan:** no tests — code review + observation ticket
**Completion criteria:** Grep for direct cortex writes vs Librarian tool calls; confirm Librarian edge maintenance runs on Librarian's lifecycle. Record: wired / partially wired / not wired.
